caveats: a: OEWS combines wired telephone apparatus with wireless and broadcast communications equipment.
a: Large carrier-equipment vendors differ from LMM enterprise and specialty network-equipment makers.
rho: Network performance and revenue gains are not all labor substitution.
rho: Carrier-grade access equipment and small-business premises devices have different implementation thresholds.
e: Establishments are not firms.
e: The 25.32% Computers/Peripherals margin bridge may overstate EBITDA for low-margin equipment vendors and understate service-supported firms.

s5: Asset and product-line sales may not transfer an eligible operating firm.

q: Routers, PBX systems, gateways, access gear, and analog-replacement devices have different pricing power.

envelope.interval_scope: research-input sensitivity with dataset anchors (l, n) held fixed

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Repeat equipment, platform, license, support, and service sales to carriers, broadband operators, enterprises, governments, utilities, municipalities, distributors, and integrators, priced by port, capacity, feature, configuration, volume, qualification, support, and multi-year procurement terms.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive plants, cellular handset makers, wireless radio equipment, pure software and telecom services, distributors, installers without manufacturing, component makers, passive entities, and firms without transferable operations.
lens.included_activities: Independent U.S. firms repeatedly designing, assembling, manufacturing, testing, and selling wired telephone and data-communications equipment such as switches, PBX systems, routers, gateways, bridges, modems, and customer-premises access equipment to external customers.

a.bridge: BLS measures broader 334200 and reports 16.90% of employment in computer occupations and 14.22% in management, alongside engineering, sales, office, production, maintenance, and logistics. NIST documents manufacturing AI uses in quality, maintenance, forecasting, scheduling, and resource management. I translate this broader high-skill mix into wage-weighted remaining exposure for wired network-equipment makers; extensive software tooling and factory automation reduce the incremental share.
caveats: OEWS combines wired telephone apparatus with wireless and broadcast communications equipment.
Large carrier-equipment vendors differ from LMM enterprise and specialty network-equipment makers.
a.change_evidence: A 334210 task-and-wage study separating existing automation from remaining engineering, test, product, sales, support, production, supply-chain, and administrative hours would materially change the range.
a.rationale: AI can assist hardware and firmware engineering, configuration, test analysis, network simulation, defect diagnosis, documentation, forecasting, sourcing, proposals, support, and administration. Physical assembly, rework, certification, cybersecurity assurance, field interoperability, and accountable release remain.

d5.bridge: BLS projects broader communications-equipment real output from $54.5 billion in 2024 to $66.5 billion in 2034, a 2.0% annual rate. ADTRAN reports 2025 revenue up 17.5% after normalized spending, fiber expansion, vendor consolidation, critical-infrastructure upgrades, and AI-related bandwidth demand. I convert broader output and current network-investment evidence into a five-year constant-quality ratio; rebound conditions and wireless products in 334200 can overstate sustained 334210 growth.
caveats: BLS 334200 includes wireless and broadcast equipment and measures real value rather than physical units.
ADTRAN's 2025 growth followed a spending normalization and may not persist.
d5.change_evidence: A five-year Dell'Oro or Omdia U.S. shipment forecast for routing, switching, broadband access, PBX, gateways, modems, and POTS-replacement hardware, reconciled to domestic manufacture, would materially improve the estimate.
d5.rationale: Fiber upgrades, cloud and AI traffic, IoT, video, edge computing, cybersecurity, POTS replacement, and network modernization support demand. Virtualization, cloud-managed software, wireless substitution, carrier capital cycles, consolidation, and offshore production create downside.

caveats: Establishments are not firms.
The 25.32% Computers/Peripherals margin bridge may overstate EBITDA for low-margin equipment vendors and understate service-supported firms.
e.change_evidence: A named review of the 47 candidates covering ultimate ownership, domestic manufacturing substance, external repeat revenue, normalized EBITDA, hardware versus software mix, customer concentration, certifications, and transferable IP would directly measure eligibility.
e.rationale: Census reports 161 employer establishments and the frozen count identifies 47 firms in the EBITDA band, plausibly including independent access, gateway, PBX, enterprise-network, and specialty-equipment makers. Large public groups, captive units, software-heavy vendors, importers, contract-manufacturing-light brands, weak-margin firms, and concentrated carrier suppliers reduce eligibility.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 334210 ($2,141,537,452) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 334210 ($5,404,782,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

n.detail: margin 0.2532 (margins.json[334]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Computers/Peripherals EBITDA/Sales 25.32%); revenue/employee $599,865 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 334210); class <5: 55 firms, avg revenue/firm $673,782 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $170,602 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 26 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,079,846 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $526,617 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $6,940,769 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,757,403 -> IN $1-10M band; class 20-99: 34 firms, avg revenue/firm $15,778,824 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $3,995,198 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 14 firms, avg revenue/firm $68,949,357 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $17,457,977 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 15 firms, avg revenue/firm $248,109,800 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $62,821,401 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 47

caveats: Wireless substitution and imports can displace domestic operators without making software the hardware supplier.
Virtualization shifts value toward software and is partly reflected in d5.
o.change_evidence: Evidence of rapid white-box self-integration, software-defined substitution eliminating dedicated categories, or autonomous contract production removing the lens operator would lower the estimate.
o.rationale: Remaining wired-network demand requires an accountable maker to design or integrate, test, certify, secure, warrant, and deliver physical equipment. Virtualization and cloud-managed architectures can eliminate some dedicated appliances, but remaining ports, access devices, and gateways still require hardware operators.

q.bridge: ADTRAN reports service-provider customers, distributor channels, customer concentration, competitive network investment, and demand for cost-effective migration; Harmonic describes hardware-centric network costs and movement toward software-driven architectures. I translate these vendor disclosures to LMM wired-equipment makers. Large buyer procurement and standards increase pass-through, while qualification, interoperability, embedded software, support, and installed-base switching costs protect retention.
caveats: ADTRAN and Harmonic are larger and more international than the target lens.
Routers, PBX systems, gateways, access gear, and analog-replacement devices have different pricing power.
q.change_evidence: Eligible-firm contracts, bid tabs, discount and rebate histories, support attach rates, renewal pricing, customer concentration, and gross-margin persistence after automation would materially change the range.
q.rationale: Carrier and enterprise bids, standardized interfaces, vendor consolidation, component transparency, and powerful customers force price sharing. Proprietary management software, reliability, security, support, certification, and rapid deployment preserve some gains.

caveats: Network performance and revenue gains are not all labor substitution.
Carrier-grade access equipment and small-business premises devices have different implementation thresholds.
rho.change_evidence: Audited deployments reporting engineering cycle, test coverage, defect escapes, configuration errors, forecast accuracy, support resolution, overrides, outages, and labor hours saved would narrow the estimate.
rho.rationale: Code, schematics, test traces, network telemetry, support tickets, and configuration data favor implementation. Interoperability, cybersecurity, export controls, carrier qualification, long product lives, sparse field failures, legacy systems, and high outage costs constrain autonomous use.

s5.bridge: SBA reports that 47% of family-business owners expecting retirement within five years lack a successor. I use it as broad succession evidence and discount it because communications-equipment firms often pursue strategic mergers, product-line sales, recapitalizations, or closures rather than qualifying transfers, and many are corporate rather than family-owned.
caveats: The SBA source is cross-industry and not a transaction rate.
Asset and product-line sales may not transfer an eligible operating firm.
s5.change_evidence: PitchBook, GF Data, Axial, and sector transaction records matched to eligible 334210 firms, plus owner-age and succession interviews, would replace the proxy.
s5.rationale: Founder-led niche network-equipment makers can sell due to succession, strategic consolidation, or capital needs, while carrier qualification, customer concentration, founder-dependent IP, security obligations, and technology transitions can prevent transfer.

principal_driver: Bandwidth growth and digitized engineering, test, configuration, and support workflows create implementable AI opportunity.
principal_weakness: Powerful carrier buyers and software-driven architectures can compress both retained savings and dedicated-hardware demand.
